_meta.description: oh-my-mlip public MLIP registry. A user's agent (or oh_my_mlip.provider) reads this to resolve an env, its interpreter, and the import/inference code for each model. Public edition of the internal catbench roster.
_meta.source_policy: Each framework's env+model is unified to the fastest validated setup. Same checkpoint = same accuracy; only speed differs across contributors.

field_guide.env: conda env name; the relocatable env unpacks to ${OH_MY_MLIP_HOME}/envs/<env>
field_guide.default_version: optional; for a multi-version framework, the version key resolve() selects when version=None. Must be one of the framework's version keys.
field_guide.python: interpreter path of that framework's env, ${OH_MY_MLIP_HOME}-relative (do NOT use conda activate)
field_guide.import: import lines for the top of a generated run script
field_guide.arch_pinned: if true, pick the inference_<arch> variant matching the host GPU (sm86=A5000/A6000, sm89=L40S, ...; auto-detected via registry.detect_host_arch). A generic 'inference' block may carry the ${OMM_ARCH} placeholder, which resolve() expands to the arch in use — so ANY host arch resolves and the per-arch .pt2 is compiled on the user GPU on first run, never baked into a distributed tarball
field_guide.status: free-form provenance/validation provenance string carried verbatim from the internal registry
field_guide.env_run: optional env-var prefix that MUST wrap the interpreter invocation (key=value allowlist only; e.g. LD_LIBRARY_PATH="")
field_guide.gated: bool. if true, weights require accepting an upstream license and using the user's own HF_TOKEN; gated weights are NEVER redistributed by this repo
field_guide.license_url: string|null. URL of the upstream weights license to accept before download (set for gated models)
field_guide.weights: how weights are obtained: 'bundled' (inside the conda-pack tarball) | 'auto-download' (fetched by name to a shared cache on first run) | 'on-demand-hf' (fetched from Hugging Face Hub, may be gated)
field_guide.weights_fetch: fetch mechanism: 'by-name' (resolved by model name/cache) | 'url' (explicit download URL) | 'gated-hf' (gated HF repo requiring license acceptance + HF_TOKEN)
field_guide.weights_source: the source value matching weights_fetch: an HF repo id (owner/name) for gated-hf, an http(s) URL (or the 'TODO-owner-review' placeholder, filled with the real official link by a later goal) for url, a resolver name/placeholder for by-name
field_guide.weights_fetch_command: optional argv template for by-name weights that still need an explicit local models/<env>/ path; {weights_source} and {target_root} are available
field_guide.weights_cache_env: optional env var set to the target root before running weights_fetch_command
field_guide.validation: host-GPU validation state, TWO INDEPENDENT AXES (do not conflate). ARCH-VALIDITY: 'validated_sm86' | 'validated_sm89' means the compiled/AOT artifact is valid for that compute capability; this transfers across same-arch hosts (e.g. an sm89 value is arch-valid on both L40S and RTX 4060 Ti). HOST-RESOURCE/DRIVER-VALIDITY: a separate concern (VRAM ceiling, CUDA driver<->runtime skew) that does NOT transfer and must be (re)checked on the user's own host. A 'validated_sm89' value carried over from the internal catbench L40S host is an ARCH claim only; on a different sm89 host it may still fail tier-2 for host-resource/driver reasons, in which case CR-1 applies (tier-1 via CPU PASS, tier-2 honest degrade). Other states: 'gpu_pending' (env/load OK but GPU single-point and/or D3 not yet verified on any host) | 'cpu_only'. Source of truth for a given host is that host's measured tier-1/tier-2 result, not a carried-over arch label.

sm89.host_note: RTX 4060 Ti (16GB, compute cap 8.9) is the local WSL self-healing host; it is arch-compatible with L40S sm89 artifacts but has a smaller 16GB VRAM ceiling and may hit host-resource/driver-validity limits independent of arch-validity (see field_guide.validation).

_meta.catbench: All envs ship catbench. D3: from catbench.dispersion import DispersionCorrection; calc = DispersionCorrection().apply(calc). The arch-specific pair_d3.so is NOT baked into distributed tarballs; it recompiles on the user GPU on first run (needs nvcc; see env.sh).
_meta.note: v1 authors the MACE + SevenNet distribution pipeline; their tarballs are upload-pending the compute checkpoint (build+publish + foreign-host end-to-end not yet run). All other rows are carried at their TRUE validation state; rows marked validation='gpu_pending' are env/load-verified only and are Phase-2 packaging targets.

accel.install: pip install sevenn[oeq]  (openequivariance; or sevenn[cueq12]/sevenn[cueq13] for cuEquivariance by CUDA major)
accel.load_note: enable at calculator construction: SevenNetCalculator(..., enable_oeq=True)  (or --enable_oeq / --enable_cueq on the sevenn CLI)

SevenNet-MF-OMPA.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): single_point.py SevenNet energy=-16.389599 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 121MB/max 333MB (torch 2.7.1+cu126). enable_oeq=True succeeds against the cuequivariance 0.8.0 wheels in envs/sevennet.yml (no separate openequivariance install needed).

SevenNet-Omni.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-07-01): single_point.py SevenNet --version SevenNet-Omni energy=-16.384132 eV (torch 2.7.1+cu126, cuda_available=True).

MACE-MH-1-OMAT.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-07-01): single_point.py MACE --version MACE-MH-1-OMAT energy=-14.969970 eV (torch 2.7.1+cu126, cuda_available=True); head='omat_pbe', weight 'mh-1' auto-downloads.

MACE-MH-1-OC20.note: MACE-MH-1 is multi-head; the OC20/catalysis head is 'oc20_usemppbe' (PBE surfaces), not 'oc20'. tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-07-01): single_point.py MACE --version MACE-MH-1-OC20 energy=-12.935509 eV (torch 2.7.1+cu126).

NequIP.note: arch-pinned. Pick sm86/sm89 by host GPU. NequIPCalculator ASE path is from_compiled_model ONLY (no uncompiled load) -> the per-arch .pt2 is required, not optional; it is reselected/recompiled on the user GPU, never baked into a distributed tarball. HOST-VERIFIED tier-2 (RTX 4060 Ti sm89, 2026-06-30) for NequIP-OAM-L: nequip-compile produced models/compiled/sm89/NequIP-OAM-L_sm89.nequip.pt2 (aotinductor, torch 2.9.1+cu128), single_point.py NequIP --version NequIP-OAM-L energy=-16.379920 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 8.5MB/max 47MB. RUNTIME: the .pt2 imports openequivariance, whose extension JIT-loads via torch cpp_extension -> needs `ninja` on PATH (now injected per-env by the provider from <env>/bin) + nvrtc.h on CPATH (now set by env.sh section 3 from CUDA_HOME/include); a cold cache recompiles once, then loads from ~/.cache/torch_extensions. default_version is NequIP-OAM-XL: its sm89 .pt2 must be compiled the same way before `single_point.py NequIP` (no --version) works.

accel.load_note: import openequivariance; NequIPCalculator.from_compiled_model(<file>, device='cuda')  (use a torchscript .nequip.pth for torch<2.10; LAMMPS deploy: nequip-prepare-lmp-mliap)

NequIP-OAM-XL.note: sm89 .pt2 host-verified tier-2 on RTX 4060 Ti (2026-06-30): default single_point.py NequIP energy=-16.386003 eV; nequip-compile produced models/compiled/sm89/NequIP-OAM-XL_sm89.nequip.pt2 (aotinductor). See framework note for the oeq runtime/compile flow. BETA-TEST (RTX A4500 sm86, 2026-07): VERIFY-BLOCKED — no sm86 .pt2 is shipped and install.sh does not compile one, and nequip-compile with enable_OpenEquivariance hung deterministically at model-load there (Allegro's cueq compile succeeded on the same host). The launcher now auto-detects the host arch (registry.detect_host_arch) and passes --arch to the worker instead of silently defaulting sm89. ROOT CAUSE (2026-07-16, oeq v0.4.1 upstream dissection): importing openequivariance JIT-builds two torch cpp_extension modules (extlib/__init__.py) with ninja at MAX_JOBS=nproc, and torch's FileBaton spin-waits forever on a stale build lock left by a killed first build — on a pids-limited host the first build dies and every later load hangs silently. Remedies shipped: env_run MAX_JOBS=4 (caps the worker's first-run JIT), compile_nequip.sh preflight (MAX_JOBS default + stale-lock reap), and env.sh's prebuilt torch_ext seeding. cueq (Allegro) is immune: prebuilt kernels, no JIT. oeq 0.6.8 TESTED (2026-07-18, upstream's suggestion on issue #207): import is AOT (~1.6 s, no JIT -> hang class gone), BUT 0.4.1-compiled .pt2 artifacts FAIL to load under it (TorchJITConv class renamed) — bumping the pin requires recompiling every per-arch .pt2 + re-verifying vs /TGM; its sdist CMake build also needs a FULL CUDA toolkit (set CUDAToolkit_ROOT; conda cuda-nvcc alone lacks CUDA::nvrtc). Pin stays 0.4.1 with the MAX_JOBS/lock guards until that recompile-and-reverify is done.

NequIP-OAM-L.note: sm89 .pt2 host-verified tier-2 on RTX 4060 Ti (energy=-16.379920 eV); see framework note for the oeq runtime/compile flow.

Allegro.note: arch-pinned. HOST-VERIFIED tier-2 (RTX 4060 Ti sm89, 2026-06-30): import is cuequivariance_torch (NOT openequivariance — Allegro is a cueq-backed model). nequip-compile produced models/compiled/sm89/Allegro-OAM-L_sm89.nequip.pt2 (aotinductor, torch 2.8.0+cu128, NO oeq modifier), single_point.py Allegro energy=-16.384536 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 8.5MB/max 101MB. The cueq ops ship as wheels (cuequivariance-ops-cu12) so there is no runtime JIT/ninja step; the provider's per-env PATH injection + env.sh CPATH still apply harmlessly. NequIPCalculator ASE path is from_compiled_model ONLY; the per-arch .pt2 is reselected/recompiled on the user GPU, never shipped.

accel.install: cuequivariance + cuequivariance-torch + cuequivariance-ops-cu12/-torch-cu12 are pinned in envs/allegro.yml (no separate install); they provide the tensor_product op schemas the AOT .pt2 needs at load.
accel.load_note: import cuequivariance_torch; NequIPCalculator.from_compiled_model(<file>, device='cuda')  (use a torchscript .nequip.pth for torch<2.10; do NOT add --modifiers enable_OpenEquivariance — that is NequIP-only)

accel_lammps.install: git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/lammps/lammps && git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/mir-group/pair_nequip_allegro && ./pair_nequip_allegro/patch_lammps.sh /path/to/lammps/  (then build LAMMPS with the MLIAP/KOKKOS C++ cmake; no pip path)
accel_lammps.load_note: LAMMPS pair_style mliap; the C++ build is per host/GPU (cmake), not a pip wheel.

Allegro-OAM-L.note: sm89 .pt2 host-verified tier-2 on RTX 4060 Ti (energy=-16.384536 eV, cueq backend); see framework note for compile/runtime flow. BETA-TEST (RTX A4500 sm86, 2026-07): sm86 .pt2 compiled on-host (cueq, ~90 s), PASS-GPU energy=-16.384530 eV — but only via direct from_compiled_model, because the launcher then defaulted to sm89; fixed 2026-07-16 (host-arch auto-detect + --arch passthrough).

Nequix.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30) via the JAX backend: NequixCalculator(model_path=...nequix-mp-1.nqx, backend='jax', use_kernel=False) energy=-16.378962 eV on jax.devices()=[CudaDevice(id=0)]. KEY: nequix is a PyPI package (`pip install nequix`) whose DEFAULT backend is jax with the native .nqx checkpoint; OpenEquivariance is OPTIONAL (use_kernel=False -> e3nn-jax tensor products, no oeq build needed). Earlier torch-backend attempts failed because the published .nqx is JAX-native (torch.load can't deserialize it) and oeq 0.6.4 sdist won't build — both AVOIDED by the jax+use_kernel=False path. The local weight MUST keep the .nqx extension (a .pt name makes the loader attempt a torch->jax conversion needing torch e3nn).

Nequix-MP-1.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-07-01): single_point.py Nequix energy=-16.378962 eV via the JAX backend (backend='jax', use_kernel=False -> e3nn-jax; OpenEquivariance omitted). NOT in /TGM -> no bit-exact reference; runs+energy on host is the oracle. Matches the recipe build_note. Recipe python bumped 3.10.20 -> 3.11.13 (2026-07-16, R2/cp310 wheel portability): rebuilt + re-verified on this host, energy identical (-16.378962 eV).

DeePMD.note: MUST run with LD_LIBRARY_PATH='' (env_run) to avoid a system Intel oneAPI libfabric / mpich FABRIC_1.8 clash at deepmd import (beta-test finding, 2026-07: DPA4 already carried this env_run — which is exactly why DPA4 stayed clean and DeePMD needed a manual workaround).

DPA-3.1-3M-FT.note: DPA-3.1-3M.pt is a multi-task TRAINING checkpoint (~31 domain heads); DeePMD's DP/DeepPot needs a single-head FROZEN model. prepare_deepmd_weights.py downloads the checkpoint and `dp --pt freeze --head Omat24` it (OMat24 head, consistent with the OAM roster) into frozen-omat24.pth before inference. prepare_deepmd_weights.py is wired into install.sh (runs with the ENV interpreter, --target-root models/deepmd) so a fresh build auto-freezes; the helper resolves `dp` next to sys.executable (works without activating the env). tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-07-01): single_point.py DeePMD energy=-14.992299 eV (torch 2.8.0+cu128, cuda_available=True); energy reproduces bit-identically across a from-scratch download+freeze. Recipe python bumped 3.10.19 -> 3.11.13 (2026-07-16, R2/cp310 wheel portability): rebuilt + re-verified on this host, energy bit-identical (-14.992299 eV, torch 2.8.0+cu128).

ORB.note: Confirmed: orb_v3_conservative_inf_omat + precision='float32-high' (matches jumoon reference exactly). conservative+inf is CPU-neighbor-list heavy; speed varies with host CPU load.

ORB-v3.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): single_point.py ORB energy=-14.968285 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 119MB/max 141MB (torch 2.7.1+cu126, orb-models 0.5.4).

GRACE-2L-OAM.note: grace_models download lands a TF SavedModel in a NESTED layout (<target>/<name>/<name>/saved_model.pb); the registry inference points TPCalculator at <target> directly. prepare_grace_weights.py runs grace_models then flattens the dir so saved_model.pb sits at models/grace/GRACE-2L-OAM/. No weights_sha256 (target is a multi-file SavedModel directory, not a single artifact). weights_size is the saved_model.pb size only. prepare_grace_weights.py is wired into install.sh (env interpreter, uniform --target-root models/grace -> target-dir models/grace/GRACE-2L-OAM). tier host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti (2026-07-01): single_point.py GRACE energy=-16.391527 eV (TensorFlow backend; D3 unavailable as GRACE has no torch, MLIP still runs). Beta-test hardening (2026-07): grace_models 0.5.3 downloads into ~/.cache/grace (ignores cwd) — prepare_grace_weights.py now falls back to that cache (and $GRACE_CACHE); on old-libstdc++ hosts scipy needs CXXABI_1.3.15 — the worker prepends envs/grace/lib to LD_LIBRARY_PATH.

MatterSim-v1-5M.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): single_point.py MatterSim energy=-16.389614 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 108MB/max 163MB (torch 2.6.0+cu124). Phase-0 bucket-D (cpu_fallback) resolved: the inference forces device='cuda' so it runs on GPU (not CPU).

CHGNet-v0.3.0.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): single_point.py CHGNet energy=-16.333233 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 18.7MB/max 88MB (torch 2.7.1+cu126). CHGNetCalculator auto-selects GPU when available; pass use_device='cuda' to force.

AlphaNet.note: verify ckpt_path and config json filename (jumoon pretrained alex_0410.ckpt + oma.json). Public edition (github @65f8ea93) accepted 2026-07-01: GPU-verified (validation=validated_sm89); energy+forces correct and slab bit-identical vs /TGM, but the gas-phase (isolated-molecule) path differs -> NOT bit-exact to /TGM (see version note).

AlphaNet-v1-OMA.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): single_point.py AlphaNet energy=-16.406492 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 36MB/max 81MB. Built via envs/alphanet.build.sh (install.sh auto-runs it): AlphaNet pinned to public github.com/zmyybc/AlphaNet@65f8ea93 (--no-deps) + torch 2.1.2+cu121 + numpy==1.26.4 + pydantic_settings/pydantic/rich/scikit-learn; oma.json config from the repo's pretrained/OMA/oma.json, weight alex_0410.ckpt from figshare 53851139 (sha256 879a477.. matches). ACCURACY CAVEAT: this public build computes energy+forces correctly (tier PASS) but gas-molecule energies differ from the internal /TGM build (slab bit-identical; gas up to 0.24 eV/atom, H2 ~0.49 eV) — the accepted public edition is NOT bit-exact to /TGM for gas-phase (owner-accepted 2026-07-01; promoted to build_status=clean). Do NOT install matscipy (forces numpy>=2, breaks torch 2.1.2 ABI). prestage_alphanet_weights.py (install.sh hook) stages BOTH alex_0410.ckpt (sha-verified) and the pinned-SHA oma.json up front — beta-test (2026-07) found oma.json was never auto-fetched, breaking single_point on fresh clones.

Eqnorm-MPtrj.note: tier-2 GPU re-confirmed (energy=-16.365646 eV, CUDA alloc 66MB on RTX 4060 Ti). Weight cache: the eqnorm package downloads to ~/.cache/eqnorm/eqnorm-mptrj.pt via `wget` against figshare.com/files/55429685, which 202-blocks on this host (leaves a 0-byte file -> EOFError 'Ran out of input' on torch.load, and the package then SKIPS re-download because the file 'exists'). Recovery: rm the 0-byte cache and fetch via the working subdomain `curl -L https://ndownloader.figshare.com/files/55429685 -o ~/.cache/eqnorm/eqnorm-mptrj.pt` (sha256 9fd5b97.. verifies). Inference has no models/ local target so fetch.py's figshare normalization does not apply to eqnorm's in-package downloader.

fairchemv1.note: eSEN-30M-OAM works only via fairchem v1 OCPCalculator. Checkpoint esen_30m_oam.pt.

eSEN-30M-OAM.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified GATED PASS on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): single_point.py fairchemv1 energy=-16.375267 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 140MB/max 518MB; weight esen_30m_oam.pt (362285045 B) fetched from gated facebook/OMAT24. WORKING CREDENTIAL = the huggingface-cli cached token at ~/.cache/huggingface/token (account JinukMoon, which has accepted the facebook/OMAT24 license); NOTE the repo's huggging_token file is a STALE/INVALID token (whoami fails) — do not use it. honest-fail path also verified: NO token -> clean GatedError surfacing license_url + docs/hf_token.md (AGENTS §5). Because env.sh redirects HF_HOME to models/hf, the cli-cached token is not auto-discovered; pass it via HF_TOKEN or HF_TOKEN_PATH. CROSS-HOST: beta-test (RTX A4500 sm86, 2026-07-07) PASS-GPU-FRESH with the user's own token — energy=-16.375267 eV, gated weight sha256 matched the recorded fingerprint.

EquiformerV3.note: OCPCalculator + checkpoint_path. MUST pass cpu=False — OCPCalculator defaults cpu=True so without it the model runs on CPU (no GPU, extremely slow). fairchem-core dev6 is an editable install; install.sh recreates it (no baked editable .pth in distributed tarballs). No OC20-trained checkpoint on HF yet (4 available). Owner-approved 2026-07-01: public sha a7300c58 pinned, GPU-verified (validation=validated_sm89), reproduces /TGM bit-identical (build_status=clean).

EqV3-OMatMPtrjSalex.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): single_point.py EquiformerV3 energy=-16.392015 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 140MB/max 752MB (torch 2.7.1+cu128). Built via the vendored fairchem-core (envs/equiformer_v3.yml sha a7300c58) which provides OCPCalculator + the EqV3 model; recipe now pins scipy==1.16.0 (>=1.17 removed scipy.special.sph_harm used by fairchem gemnet layers). HF file lives under a checkpoint/ prefix: checkpoint/omat24-mptrj-salex_gradient.pt (the bare filename 404s). Owner-approved 2026-07-01: recipe promoted to build_status=clean; the immutable public sha a7300c58 is pinned with owner approval (reproduces /TGM bit-identical).

UMA.model_source: name-based auto-download from Hugging Face (gated; requires accepting the Meta FAIR UMA license and using the user's own HF_TOKEN)
UMA.note: get_predict_unit + FAIRChemCalculator(task_name). task_name = oc20/omat/oc22/oc25 per variant. GATED: weights are NOT redistributed by this repo; the user must accept the upstream license and provide HF_TOKEN. tier-2 GPU host-verified GATED PASS on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): default UMA-s-1p2-OMAT (get_predict_unit('uma-s-1p2', task_name='omat')) energy=-15.006906 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 1183MB/max 1214MB (torch 2.8.0+cu128, fairchem-core 2.16.0). WORKING CREDENTIAL = the huggingface-cli cached token ~/.cache/huggingface/token (account JinukMoon, accepted the facebook/UMA license); the repo's huggging_token file is STALE/INVALID — pass the valid token via HF_TOKEN/HF_TOKEN_PATH (env.sh redirects HF_HOME so the cli token isn't auto-discovered). honest-fail (no token) also verified via the shared gated-hf path (eSEN). NOTE: the UMA checkpoint download is ~2.8GB and can stall on a slow network (a first attempt hung at 602MB); just rerun. Other UMA variants reuse this path with their task_name.

UMA-m-1p1-OC20.note: CAUTION (beta-test, RTX A4500 sm86 host, 2026-07): the 11.2 GB uma-m-1p1.pt checkpoint stalled deterministically at predictor/model construction — checkpoint verified complete (download ruled out), RAM/VRAM ample (OOM ruled out), GPU never engaged; reproduced as an unbounded >5 h hang, then a bounded 20-min cap. All -s- (small) UMA variants load fine. Budget/monitor the first uma-m load; not re-verified on this repo's 4060 Ti host.

UMA-m-1p1-OMAT.note: Shares the exact uma-m-1p1 checkpoint/load path with UMA-m-1p1-OC20 (only task_name differs) — see that variant's model-load stall CAUTION before first use.

PET.model_source: ${OH_MY_MLIP_HOME}/models/pet/pet-oam-xl-v1.0.0.pt (exported from the HF lab-cosmo/upet .ckpt via `mtt export`; the repo publishes only a .ckpt)
PET.status: clone + D3(.so) + load + GPU single-point verified (MetatomicCalculator, .pt exported from .ckpt).
PET.note: Calculator moved metatensor.torch.atomistic -> metatomic.torch.ase_calculator. MetatomicCalculator needs an EXPORTED metatomic .pt, NOT the raw training .ckpt; scripts/prepare_pet_weights.py downloads the .ckpt and `mtt export`s it to .pt.

PET-OAM-XL.note: Materialized by prepare_pet_weights.py: download pet-oam-xl-v1.0.0.ckpt (HF lab-cosmo/upet) then `mtt export` -> pet-oam-xl-v1.0.0.pt. No weights_sha256: the exported .pt is not byte-reproducible across export runs (each `mtt export` re-serializes); the owner's validated .pt was 2921011063 bytes, ours is 2921010871 — same model, different serialization. weights_size is our local export. prepare_pet_weights.py is wired into install.sh (env interpreter, --target-root models/pet; resolves `mtt` next to sys.executable). tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-07-01): single_point.py PET energy=-16.394783 eV (torch 2.9.1+cu128, cuda_available=True).

EquFlash.backend_note: EquFlashV2 architecture is cueq-only (FullConv uses cuequivariance exclusively; no flashtp backend in v2 code). FlashTP was compiled for sm86;sm89 but applies only to EquFlash v1, not v2.
EquFlash.status: GPU single-point + D3 verified. EquFlashV2 runs on cueq.
EquFlash.note: Use cueq (default). Do NOT inject conv_type='flashtp' — v2 FullConv rejects it (TypeError). flashtp is a v1-only feature.

EquFlashV2.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): EquFlashV2 (cueq, 44.9M params) energy=-16.391567 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated=200097792 B (~191MB), max 285MB. Weight EquFlashV2.pt (898917921 B, sha256 068ce25.. matches) fetched via figshare; fetch.py rewrites the figshare file URL to the ndownloader.figshare.com subdomain (the bare host 202-blocks). ENV BUILD: a single `conda env create` from equflash.yml is a pip ResolutionImpossible (fairchem-core 1.10.0 vs torch 2.9.1+cu126); install.sh auto-runs the multi-pass sidecar envs/equflash.build.sh (2-pass pip: torch+PyG+cueq+GGNN, then fairchem --no-deps + GGNN runtime deps).

EquFlash.note: tier-2 GPU host-verified on RTX 4060 Ti sm89 (2026-06-30): EquFlash v1 energy=-16.398239 eV, torch.cuda.memory_allocated 133MB/max 223MB. Runs on the existing equflash env via the DEFAULT backend (cueq) — the flashtp accel below is OPTIONAL/GPU-unverified and NOT required for this single-point PASS. Weight EquFlash.pt (345648259 B, sha256 4a1eb19.. matches) fetched via the figshare subdomain fix.

accel.install: git clone https://github.com/SNU-ARC/flashTP.git && cd flashTP && pip install -r requirements.txt && CUDA_ARCH_LIST="80;90" pip install . --no-build-isolation
accel.load_note: v1-only flashtp backend (importable module flashTP_e3nn); select via conv_type='flashtp'. EquFlashV2 is cueq-only and rejects this.

MatRIS.model_source: name-based auto-download to ~/.cache/matris (pre-fetched MatRIS_10M_OAM.pth.tar via figshare)
MatRIS.status: tier-1 CPU PASS host-verified (RTX 4060 Ti host, 2026-06-30): energy=-16.386652 eV (device='cpu'). Two issues fixed/diagnosed: (A) weight cache ~/.cache/matris/MatRIS_10M_OAM.pth.tar was 0-byte (matris downloads matris/model/model.py DOWNLOAD_URLS figshare.com/ndownloader/files/59142728 which 202-blocks on this host -> EOFError 'Ran out of input'); recovery: rm cache + curl https://ndownloader.figshare.com/files/59142728 (sha256 c033abc5.., 42273174 B). matris uses its own torch.hub downloader so fetch.py figshare normalization does not apply. (E) tier-2 GPU DEGRADE (driver skew, CR-1): torch 2.12.1+cu130 needs a CUDA-13 driver; host is 12.9 -> force device='cpu'.

MatRIS-10M-OAM.note: tier-1 CPU PASS on the RTX 4060 Ti box (2026-06-30): builds + imports + computes energy/forces once on CPU. GPU needs a CUDA-13-class driver (torch 2.12.1+cu130); the CUDA-12.8 host lacks it, so it ran CPU (validation=tier1_cpu_driver_skew). WEIGHTS: MatRISCalculator(model='matris_10m_oam') auto-downloads inside the matris package; on hosts where its upstream endpoint 202-blocks, it writes a 0-byte file -> EOFError on load (same class as eqnorm's in-package figshare download). weights_sha256 c033abc5.. (42273174 B) is now recorded; prestage_matris_weights.py verifies it warn-only (a mismatch logs a warning but does NOT fail, since the upstream figshare file may be re-published). MatRIS is NOT in /TGM, so there is no reference checkpoint to compare against; that plus GPU driver-skew (GPU unverified on this CUDA-12.8 host) keeps the recipe candidate. CPU build+run is verified (energy=-16.386652 eV). CROSS-HOST GPU PROOF (beta-test, RTX A4500 sm86, driver 580.82.07 / CUDA 13.0, 2026-07): PASS-GPU-FRESH, energy=-16.386652 eV on GPU (1746 MiB) — identical to the CPU value here; the tier1_cpu_driver_skew label is host-driver-dependent (a CUDA-13 driver resolves it), not intrinsic to the model.

DPA4.status: env+ckpt+load OK (CPU). GPU single-point + D3 PENDING.
DPA4.note: MUST run with LD_LIBRARY_PATH='' (env_run) to avoid Intel oneAPI libfabric clash (FABRIC_1.8 not found). mpich required by 3.2.0b0 (no try/except skip).

DPA-4.0.1-pro-MPtrj.note: Weight fetch FIXED via fetch.py figshare normalization: figshare.com/{files,ndownloader/files}/<id> 202-blocks on this host; rewritten to ndownloader.figshare.com/files/<id> (S3 redirect). Downloaded models/dpa4/dpa-4.0.1-pro-mptrj.pt (sha256 446a6e8.. matches, 273318000 bytes). tier-1 CPU PASS: DP single_point energy=-16.364233 eV. tier-2 DEGRADE (bucket E driver skew, like tace): env torch 2.11.0+cu130 needs CUDA-13 driver; host driver is 12.9 -> torch.cuda.is_available()=False -> CPU-only. MUST run with LD_LIBRARY_PATH='' (env_run) to avoid Intel oneAPI libfabric clash. CROSS-HOST GPU PROOF (beta-test, RTX A4500 sm86, CUDA 13.0 driver, 2026-07): PASS-GPU-FRESH, energy=-16.364234 eV on GPU (matches this host's CPU -16.364233 to 1e-6); the driver-skew label is host-driver-dependent.

TACE.status: tier-1 CPU PASS host-verified (RTX 4060 Ti host, 2026-06-30): energy=-16.378353 eV (device='cpu'). tier-2 GPU DEGRADE (bucket E driver skew, CR-1): torch 2.11.0+cu130 needs a CUDA-13 driver; host is 12.9 (found 12090) -> CUDA use raises 'NVIDIA driver too old'. Note torch.cuda.is_available() is unreliable here (lazy init reports True then fails on use) -> force device='cpu' on driver-skewed hosts.

TACE-OAM-L.note: tier1_cpu_driver_skew is host-driver-dependent: CROSS-HOST GPU PROOF (beta-test, RTX A4500 sm86, CUDA 13.0 driver, 2026-07) PASS-GPU energy=-16.378351 eV. On old-libstdc++ hosts (Fedora 36) the import chain (scipy.optimize._highspy) needs CXXABI_1.3.15 — the worker now prepends envs/tace/lib to LD_LIBRARY_PATH.
